{{Quote|I bet Flashpoint sure seemed easy to conquer, didn't it? Just a little research station, lightly defended…|[[Marn Hierogryph]], in a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] message to Mandalorians on Flashpoint Station|<ref name="kotor10">[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]]</ref>}}
In [[3964 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans '''attacked''' [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]], a lightly-defended [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] installation in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], as part of the initial campaigns of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Flashpoint Station was an easy target for the Mandalorians, who captured the station without much trouble. After seizing Flashpoint Station, the Mandalorians gave the installation to the infamous [[Biologist/Legends|biologist]] [[Demagol]], who used it as his base for research into [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Force power/Legends|powers]].

==Prelude==
In the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] after the end of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] faced a new enemy from beyond its borders—the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, who had carved out their own empire in the farthest reaches of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. By [[3964 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusader]] [[Army/Legends|armies]], led by [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], had begun probing the Republic's borders.<ref name="egtw" /> Preparing for a larger invasion aimed at the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], the Mandalorians soon set their sights on [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]], a Republic [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] installation within striking distance of Taris and its tributary [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. Located on the barren world of [[Flashpoint]] extremely close to its [[Flashpoint (star)|star]], Flashpoint Station was situated under an artificial [[Magnetic field/Legends|magnetic shield]] that protected it from the extreme heat and [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] generated from the nearby [[Star/Legends|star]].<ref name="kotor8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref>

==The battle==
A scientific research station, Flashpoint Station was only lightly defended and was an easy target for a Mandalorian attack. The station quickly fell to the invaders, who raised their own banners on the installation.<ref name="kotor10" />

==Aftermath==
Close to the front but largely inaccessible due to its location,<ref name="kotor8" /> Flashpoint Station was promptly given to the infamous Mandalorian [[Biologist/Legends|biologist]], [[Demagol]], who used the installation as a base for his research into the [[Force power/Legends|powers]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Over the coming [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Flashpoint Station played [[Prison/Legends|prison]] to a number of captive Jedi, who were subjected to Demagol's brutal live experiments that left many [[Death/Legends|dead]].<ref name="kotor10" /> As for the [[Mandalorian Wars|war effort]] itself, the Mandalorians waited two-and-a-half months to make their next move, beginning an ultimately [[First battle of Suurja|stalemated]] [[Fourth battle of Suurja|campaign]] to capture the planet [[Suurja]].<ref name="prime" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle for Flashpoint Station was first mentioned in ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Prime Edition]]'', an [[in-universe]] news report occurring at the end of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]], written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2006]].<ref name="prime" /> The event was referenced in [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|two]]<ref name="kotor8" /> [[Knights of the Old Republic 10|other]] [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] issues,<ref name="kotor10" /> along with the reference books ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''<ref name="tea">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> and ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''.<ref name="egtw" />
